facts.text: Hamlin CDP sits on Lake Ontario’s southern shore, bordered by Kendall to the west and Parma to the east.
State Route 18 cuts through Hamlin’s southern edge, linking the town eastward to Hilton and westward to Olcott.
The town’s name honors Hannibal Hamlin, President Lincoln’s first Vice‑President, after a 1861 renaming.
Hamlin’s early settlers arrived in 1806, with the Aretas Hascall family establishing the first permanent white homestead near Hamlin Center.
A 1848 plank road, the Brockport‑Clarkson‑Hamlin Plank Road, linked Lake Ontario to the Erie Canal and later the railroad at Brockport.
The town’s history is preserved in the North Star History Center, where Town Historian Dave Walch shares stories of Hamlin’s development.
